title: Art History Path
shortDescription: Explore a dedicated 36+ lesson art history learning path through an independent system.
fullDescription: This path introduces artists, movements, and visual culture through a dedicated lesson system. Existing students can continue in the current Art History environment without losing progress.

title: Studio Study: My Material Exploration
shortDescription: Discover the materials artists use to bring ideas to life. Explore pencils, paints, clay, paper, and other tools in this hands-on studio session. Learn how each medium has its own voice, and see how artists before 1945 experimented with texture, color, and form.
fullDescription: Discover the materials artists use to bring ideas to life. Explore pencils, paints, clay, paper, and other tools in this hands-on studio session. Learn how each medium has its own voice, and see how artists before 1945 experimented with texture, color, and form.

title: Light & Basic Forms: Sphere • Cube • Cylinder
shortDescription: Map highlight, halftone, core, reflected light, cast shadow on primitives; place edges (hard/soft/lost).
fullDescription: Map highlight, halftone, core, reflected light, cast shadow on primitives; place edges (hard/soft/lost).

title: Why We Start from Cube, Sphere, and Cylinder
shortTitle: Why We Start from Cube, Sphere, and Cylinder
shortDescription: See/construct form via planes; render a faceted ‘low-poly’ object in 4–5 value steps (no heavy blending).
fullDescription: See/construct form via planes; render a faceted ‘low-poly’ object in 4–5 value steps (no heavy blending).

title: When Geometry Meets Art — Cube & Pyramid Lab
shortDescription: Optional foundation lab using an interpenetrating rectangular prism and square pyramid to test perspective, proportion, and 3D thinking, plus an LFC mini session on why Bauhaus-style schools start from cubes and pyramids.
fullDescription: Optional foundation lab using an interpenetrating rectangular prism and square pyramid to test perspective, proportion, and 3D thinking, plus an LFC mini session on why Bauhaus-style schools start from cubes and pyramids.

refs.work: Preliminary course exercises with basic solids
Head and figure construction from simple solids

title: Measuring & Proportion Essentials
shortDescription: Use sight-size/comparative measures, angle checks, and centerlines; fix proportions before tone.
fullDescription: Use sight-size/comparative measures, angle checks, and centerlines; fix proportions before tone.

title: Tonal Rendering & Edges (Finish the Simple Still Life)
shortTitle: Tonal Rendering & Edges (Finish the Simple Still Life)
shortDescription: Group values, graduate cleanly without smudge reliance; design hard/soft/lost edges; set a background value to pop forms.
fullDescription: Group values, graduate cleanly without smudge reliance; design hard/soft/lost edges; set a background value to pop forms.

title: Perspective for Still Life: 1-Point & Ellipses
shortDescription: Keep boxes straight with horizon & VP; draw true ellipses (degree/tilt) so round objects sit on the table.
fullDescription: Keep boxes straight with horizon & VP; draw true ellipses (degree/tilt) so round objects sit on the table.

title: Surfaces & Textures: Ceramic • Metal • Glass • Fabric • Organic
shortDescription: Render material reads without over-polish: metal/glass highlights, cloth edge control, organic textures in tiles.
fullDescription: Render material reads without over-polish: metal/glass highlights, cloth edge control, organic textures in tiles.

title: Complex Still Life & Composition Design
shortDescription: Make 3 value thumbnails (notan) for 4–6 objects; choose one and take the drawing to 80% finish with clear focal path.
fullDescription: Make 3 value thumbnails (notan) for 4–6 objects; choose one and take the drawing to 80% finish with clear focal path.

title: Found Still Life (Capstone) — Setup → Portfolio Piece
shortDescription: Select objects, arrange with clear light, block-in → measure → value group → edges → finish; present with a simple process strip.
fullDescription: Select objects, arrange with clear light, block-in → measure → value group → edges → finish; present with a simple process strip.

title: Play with Spaces: Fun Creations from Everyday Objects
shortDescription: Turn everyday objects into square/triangle/circle constructions, then add one clear shadow shape. Quick, friendly value sense without heavy rules.
fullDescription: Turn everyday objects into square/triangle/circle constructions, then add one clear shadow shape. Quick, friendly value sense without heavy rules.

title: Animal Minis (No-Pressure Edition)
shortDescription: Block animal bodies with beans/ovals, add 3–4 character features, then seat each with a spot shadow. Focus on gesture over details.
fullDescription: Block animal bodies with beans/ovals, add 3–4 character features, then seat each with a spot shadow. Focus on gesture over details.

title: Found Still Life POP (Flat Color + Shadow)
shortTitle: Found Still Life POP (Flat Color + Shadow)
shortDescription: Arrange 3–5 fun objects (toy/snack/desk item). Block flat local colors, then drop one darker pass on the shadow side + a simple cast shadow under each.
fullDescription: Arrange 3–5 fun objects (toy/snack/desk item). Block flat local colors, then drop one darker pass on the shadow side + a simple cast shadow under each.

title: Cartoon Character with Light (Silhouette First)
shortDescription: Design a clean silhouette for a favourite (or original) character, then place two value spots and one crisp cast shadow. Add a single highlight for pop.
fullDescription: Design a clean silhouette for a favourite (or original) character, then place two value spots and one crisp cast shadow. Add a single highlight for pop.

title: Landscapes (Graphic Bands & Simple Shadows)
shortDescription: Divide the page into sky–middle–ground bands, place bold shapes (tree/house/path), and add short ground shadows. Think far = lighter/softer.
fullDescription: Divide the page into sky–middle–ground bands, place bold shapes (tree/house/path), and add short ground shadows. Think far = lighter/softer.

refs.work: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo (landscape bands)

title: Matisse Eyes: Bold Color, Clear Shapes
shortDescription: See like Matisse—big shapes, bold local color, confident contour. Studio: still life or bust with flat color blocks + a few decisive lines.
fullDescription: See like Matisse—big shapes, bold local color, confident contour. Studio: still life or bust with flat color blocks + a few decisive lines.

refs.work: The Dining Room in the Country

title: Poster Power: Mucha & Cappiello
shortDescription: Turn everyday objects into icons using poster logic—flat color, bold contour, and smart type placement. Studio: 4-panel colorways or one hero poster.
fullDescription: Turn everyday objects into icons using poster logic—flat color, bold contour, and smart type placement. Studio: 4-panel colorways or one hero poster.

title: Renaissance Space & Composition (Raphael · Leonardo · Michelangelo)
shortDescription: Classical space and grouping: horizon/vanishing lines + strong triangular compositions. Studio: 1-point modern hall or a triangular group portrait.
fullDescription: Classical space and grouping: horizon/vanishing lines + strong triangular compositions. Studio: 1-point modern hall or a triangular group portrait.

title: Bosch Worlds: Micro-Stories & Hybrids
shortDescription: From Bosch’s teeming details to your own tiny allegories. Studio: a 3-panel mini-triptych—ordinary / dream / hybrid creature—using line + two values.
fullDescription: From Bosch’s teeming details to your own tiny allegories. Studio: a 3-panel mini-triptych—ordinary / dream / hybrid creature—using line + two values.

title: Architecture Dialogues: Behrens × Gaudí
shortDescription: Geometric clarity vs. organic flow. Studio: hybrid façade—Behrens-like base box + Gaudí-inspired skin/pattern; optional 2-point setup.
fullDescription: Geometric clarity vs. organic flow. Studio: hybrid façade—Behrens-like base box + Gaudí-inspired skin/pattern; optional 2-point setup.

title: East Asia Focus: Song Landscapes & Ukiyo-e
shortDescription: Atmosphere and brush economy (Song shan-shui) and bold print design (ukiyo-e). Studio: ink-wash landscape or print-look poster with limited palette.
fullDescription: Atmosphere and brush economy (Song shan-shui) and bold print design (ukiyo-e). Studio: ink-wash landscape or print-look poster with limited palette.

title: Post-Impressionism Trio: Van Gogh · Gauguin · Cézanne
shortDescription: One motif, three logics—Van Gogh’s stroke rhythm, Gauguin’s flat color, Cézanne’s planes. Studio: paint or draw the same subject in each mode.
fullDescription: One motif, three logics—Van Gogh’s stroke rhythm, Gauguin’s flat color, Cézanne’s planes. Studio: paint or draw the same subject in each mode.

title: Paths to Abstraction: Malevich · Kandinsky · Mondrian
shortDescription: From reduction to pure design: abstract a reference photo into 6–10 shapes (Mondrian/Malevich logic) or a line/plane composition (Kandinsky).
fullDescription: From reduction to pure design: abstract a reference photo into 6–10 shapes (Mondrian/Malevich logic) or a line/plane composition (Kandinsky).

refs.work: Composition with Red, Blue, and Yellow

title: Bauhaus Basics: Klee & Moholy-Nagy
shortDescription: Bauhaus problem-solving with play: Klee’s color/shape grammar and Moholy-Nagy’s design experiments. Studio: 2-stage study (grayscale → color).
fullDescription: Bauhaus problem-solving with play: Klee’s color/shape grammar and Moholy-Nagy’s design experiments. Studio: 2-stage study (grayscale → color).

title: Surreal Roots: Redon & Piranesi (Convincing the Impossible)
shortDescription: Make the impossible feel real. Start with Redon’s dream imagery and Piranesi’s imagined spaces. Studio: collage three mismatched elements, then unify with shared light and one clear cast shadow.
fullDescription: Make the impossible feel real. Start with Redon’s dream imagery and Piranesi’s imagined spaces. Studio: collage three mismatched elements, then unify with shared light and one clear cast shadow.

title: Watercolor Voices: Landscape & Illustration (+ Wax Resist)
shortDescription: Transparency, flow, and edges. Make one mini landscape plus one little illustration; keep the paper white as your light; add crayon-resist highlights.
fullDescription: Transparency, flow, and edges. Make one mini landscape plus one little illustration; keep the paper white as your light; add crayon-resist highlights.

title: Acrylic Paint: Flat vs Texture (+ Acrylic Markers)
shortDescription: Explore opacity and body—flat graphic areas contrasted with one textured impasto zone. Finish with crisp acrylic-marker lines.
fullDescription: Explore opacity and body—flat graphic areas contrasted with one textured impasto zone. Finish with crisp acrylic-marker lines.

title: Graphite Studio: Tone, Edges, Control
shortDescription: Pressure control and clean value hierarchy. Lamp-lit still life; group big lights/darks first, then turn edges (hard/soft/lost).
fullDescription: Pressure control and clean value hierarchy. Lamp-lit still life; group big lights/darks first, then turn edges (hard/soft/lost).

title: Ink & Wash: Fineliner + Watercolor
shortDescription: Line authority with two gentle washes. Draw a clean silhouette (plants/street/desk), then drop shadow and mid-tone washes; keep the whites.
fullDescription: Line authority with two gentle washes. Draw a clean silhouette (plants/street/desk), then drop shadow and mid-tone washes; keep the whites.

title: Oil Pastel Landscapes: Blend & Block
shortDescription: Waxy opacity for bold color fields. Block local color, scumble a second hue, then press to blend; add dark accents last.
fullDescription: Waxy opacity for bold color fields. Block local color, scumble a second hue, then press to blend; add dark accents last.

title: Gouache Glow: Opaque Layers, Clean Shapes
shortDescription: Matte opacity and tidy edges. Monochrome under-block, then 4–6 flat color shapes with small modeling on top.
fullDescription: Matte opacity and tidy edges. Monochrome under-block, then 4–6 flat color shapes with small modeling on top.

title: Oil Painting Basics: Safer Start (Water-Mixable Oils)
shortDescription: Slow blending and rich values with a simple Zorn-style palette. Thin block-in → darks → mids → lights; big brushes first, a few crisp accents last.
fullDescription: Slow blending and rich values with a simple Zorn-style palette. Thin block-in → darks → mids → lights; big brushes first, a few crisp accents last.

title: Paint Markers: Character & Layer Logic
shortDescription: Flat graphic fills with clean edges and simple shadow logic. Design a character sheet (front pose + prop) and a ground shadow.
fullDescription: Flat graphic fills with clean edges and simple shadow logic. Design a character sheet (front pose + prop) and a ground shadow.

title: Clay & Foil Forms: From Shape to Sculpture
shortDescription: Build volume additively (air-dry clay creature or relief badge) and a quick wire-and-foil figure. Paint finishes to unify.
fullDescription: Build volume additively (air-dry clay creature or relief badge) and a quick wire-and-foil figure. Paint finishes to unify.

title: Mixed Media & Collage: Meaning by Materials (Eco Build)
shortDescription: How paper/fabric/print change the story. Make a collage panel or eco-assemblage using only reused materials; unify with selective line/paint.
fullDescription: How paper/fabric/print change the story. Make a collage panel or eco-assemblage using only reused materials; unify with selective line/paint.

title: Cafe Studio 1 — Brief & Story
shortDescription: Establish a brand story, functional checklist, and circulation intent. Outputs: 1 moodboard, 1 bubble diagram, 1 page of brand keywords.
fullDescription: Establish a brand story, functional checklist, and circulation intent. Outputs: 1 moodboard, 1 bubble diagram, 1 page of brand keywords.

title: Cafe Studio 2 — Plan Basics
shortDescription: Develop the first draft plan including outdoor seating. Outputs: 1 annotated plan with furniture and outdoor zones; optional comparison scheme.
fullDescription: Develop the first draft plan including outdoor seating. Outputs: 1 annotated plan with furniture and outdoor zones; optional comparison scheme.

title: Cafe Studio 3 — Elevation & Facade
shortDescription: Translate plan into elevations: one interior wall (bar/back wall) and one storefront façade with logo placement.
fullDescription: Translate plan into elevations: one interior wall (bar/back wall) and one storefront façade with logo placement.

title: Cafe Studio 4 — Materials & Lighting
shortDescription: Assemble a material board and a lighting diagram. Select finishes (floor, wall, counter, fabric, outdoor) and define ambient/task/accent lighting.
fullDescription: Assemble a material board and a lighting diagram. Select finishes (floor, wall, counter, fabric, outdoor) and define ambient/task/accent lighting.

title: Cafe Studio 5 — Visual Development
shortDescription: Create perspective sketches and color concepts to test atmosphere. Deliver 2–3 views (interior + façade) showing mood and material feel.
fullDescription: Create perspective sketches and color concepts to test atmosphere. Deliver 2–3 views (interior + façade) showing mood and material feel.

title: Cafe Studio 6 — Final Presentation
shortDescription: Assemble a final presentation board: plan, 2 elevations, 1–2 perspective sketches, moodboard, and materials. Present as a mini portfolio project.
fullDescription: Assemble a final presentation board: plan, 2 elevations, 1–2 perspective sketches, moodboard, and materials. Present as a mini portfolio project.

title: What Is Costume Design? (vs Fashion Design)
shortTitle: What Is Costume Design? (vs Fashion Design)
shortDescription: Scope and pipeline: costume serves story/character/time period; fashion serves brand/collection/market. Build a one-page comparison board.
fullDescription: Scope and pipeline: costume serves story/character/time period; fashion serves brand/collection/market. Build a one-page comparison board.

refs.work: Costume designs for the Ballets Russes

refs.artist: Paul Poiret / Gazette du Bon Ton

title: Figure Foundations: Proportion & Gesture
shortDescription: Adult/teen/child proportions; clear gesture lines; timed sets (10×30s → 5×2min). Deliver a proportion chart and 3 clean gestures.
fullDescription: Adult/teen/child proportions; clear gesture lines; timed sets (10×30s → 5×2min). Deliver a proportion chart and 3 clean gestures.

title: Dressing the Body I — Tops & Bottoms (Single Figure)
shortDescription: Place garments on the figure; show fit and folds (hang/pipe/zigzag). One styled look with callouts for seam and fabric behavior.
fullDescription: Place garments on the figure; show fit and folds (hang/pipe/zigzag). One styled look with callouts for seam and fabric behavior.

title: Theme Lineup — 4 Figures
shortDescription: Build a coherent mini-collection for a theme (Halloween/Christmas/Summer…). One sheet: 4-figure lineup + palette strip + 3 adjectives.
fullDescription: Build a coherent mini-collection for a theme (Halloween/Christmas/Summer…). One sheet: 4-figure lineup + palette strip + 3 adjectives.

title: Bridal Dress Studio (Hero Look)
shortDescription: Design a refined bridal costume. Front/back views plus lace/tulle/satin texture tiles; annotate fabric/trim notes.
fullDescription: Design a refined bridal costume. Front/back views plus lace/tulle/satin texture tiles; annotate fabric/trim notes.

title: Conceptual / Artistic Costume (Free Approach)
shortDescription: Push concept and shape language: moodboard → 12 silhouettes → choose 1–2 for a keyframe (character in action) with a one-sentence story.
fullDescription: Push concept and shape language: moodboard → 12 silhouettes → choose 1–2 for a keyframe (character in action) with a one-sentence story.

title: Masters on Motion: How the Greats Train
shortDescription: Study master gestures for flow and balance. 12 mini master-copies (30–60s) focusing on the line of action, weight, and silhouette read.
fullDescription: Study master gestures for flow and balance. 12 mini master-copies (30–60s) focusing on the line of action, weight, and silhouette read.

title: Principles of Dynamic Posing
shortDescription: Make poses feel alive: center of gravity, contrapposto, ribcage–pelvis ‘bean’, counter-angles, simple foreshortening. Sheet shows before/after balance corrections.
fullDescription: Make poses feel alive: center of gravity, contrapposto, ribcage–pelvis ‘bean’, counter-angles, simple foreshortening. Sheet shows before/after balance corrections.

title: Stickman → Mannequin: Adding Volume
shortDescription: Turn stick figures into 3D forms (boxes/cylinders/wedges). Convert 10 gestures; pick 1 to light with a 3-value turn (core + cast shadow).
fullDescription: Turn stick figures into 3D forms (boxes/cylinders/wedges). Convert 10 gestures; pick 1 to light with a 3-value turn (core + cast shadow).

title: Quick-Sketch Lab (30s / 60s / 2min)
shortDescription: Timed sets (20×30s, 10×60s, 6×2min). Flow → bean → a few contour accents. Curate your best 12 and annotate what worked.
fullDescription: Timed sets (20×30s, 10×60s, 6×2min). Flow → bean → a few contour accents. Curate your best 12 and annotate what worked.

title: Held Pose (5-min) + Slow-Motion Sequence
shortDescription: Combine gesture + structure in three 5-minute studies, then thumbnail a slow-motion action strip (6–9 frames) from life or video.
fullDescription: Combine gesture + structure in three 5-minute studies, then thumbnail a slow-motion action strip (6–9 frames) from life or video.

title: Figures in Motion (Foundation)
shortDescription: A 5-lesson foundation in figure sketching: learn gesture, balance, stickman-to-mannequin, quick sketches, and held poses with action strips. Includes a year-long challenge of 500 figures.
fullDescription: A 5-lesson foundation in figure sketching: learn gesture, balance, stickman-to-mannequin, quick sketches, and held poses with action strips. Includes a year-long challenge of 500 figures.

title: Illustration Voices & Your Story
seriesTitle: Illustration Lab — From Story to Finished Page
shortDescription: Explore different illustration voices (editorial, children’s book, comics, posters, concept art). Test watercolor, markers, and oil pastels. Choose a personal story + mood words; create a mood board and 12 thumbnails.
fullDescription: Explore different illustration voices (editorial, children’s book, comics, posters, concept art). Test watercolor, markers, and oil pastels. Choose a personal story + mood words; create a mood board and 12 thumbnails.

title: Storyboards & Beats (Storyline → Thumbnails)
seriesTitle: Illustration Lab — From Story to Finished Page
shortDescription: Plan 6–9 panels: setup, complication, change, resolution. Vary camera angles and panel size; build a 3-tone value map for readability. Deliverable: beat board + 2 thumbnail page plans.
fullDescription: Plan 6–9 panels: setup, complication, change, resolution. Vary camera angles and panel size; build a 3-tone value map for readability. Deliverable: beat board + 2 thumbnail page plans.

title: Composition & Atmosphere on a Page
seriesTitle: Illustration Lab — From Story to Finished Page
shortDescription: Refine layout, hierarchy, and lighting. Use thirds, leading lines, silhouettes, overlaps, and light arrows. Clean pencils and value plan. Deliverable: final page layout + atmosphere notes.
fullDescription: Refine layout, hierarchy, and lighting. Use thirds, leading lines, silhouettes, overlaps, and light arrows. Clean pencils and value plan. Deliverable: final page layout + atmosphere notes.

title: Mixed-Media Pipeline (Watercolor + Pens/Markers + Oil Pastel)
seriesTitle: Illustration Lab — From Story to Finished Page
shortDescription: Execute a safe mixed-media pipeline: waterproof linework → watercolor base → markers/pen accents → oil pastel textures. Deliverable: finished page + swatch strip of your palette.
fullDescription: Execute a safe mixed-media pipeline: waterproof linework → watercolor base → markers/pen accents → oil pastel textures. Deliverable: finished page + swatch strip of your palette.

title: Digital Finish & Publication
seriesTitle: Illustration Lab — From Story to Finished Page
shortDescription: Polish and package: scan or photo, cleanup (levels, white balance), small tweaks, captions, export as PDF/PNG. Optional GIF reveal. Deliverable: final print/web file + 3-image process strip.
fullDescription: Polish and package: scan or photo, cleanup (levels, white balance), small tweaks, captions, export as PDF/PNG. Optional GIF reveal. Deliverable: final print/web file + 3-image process strip.

title: Kickoff — Turning Fandom into Art
seriesTitle: Fan Art Arcade: Draw & Poster Lab
shortDescription: Your full demo on one simple character: reference board, style notes (eyes/hair/line weight), palette swatch, and 3 pose thumbnails. Deliverable: A5 sketch + mini style sheet.
fullDescription: Your full demo on one simple character: reference board, style notes (eyes/hair/line weight), palette swatch, and 3 pose thumbnails. Deliverable: A5 sketch + mini style sheet.

title: Block Worlds — Minecraft Logic
seriesTitle: Fan Art Arcade: Draw & Poster Lab
shortDescription: Box out figures with isometric-ish blocks; use pixel textures and a tight palette. Poster task: character + one blocky mob + item bar strip.
fullDescription: Box out figures with isometric-ish blocks; use pixel textures and a tight palette. Poster task: character + one blocky mob + item bar strip.

title: Swords & Breath — Demon Slayer Energy
seriesTitle: Fan Art Arcade: Draw & Poster Lab
shortDescription: Anime head ratios, pattern logic, hard-shade cel lighting, speed lines. Poster task: diagonal action pose + title ribbon.
fullDescription: Anime head ratios, pattern logic, hard-shade cel lighting, speed lines. Poster task: diagonal action pose + title ribbon.

title: Power Pose — Dragon Ball Impact
seriesTitle: Fan Art Arcade: Draw & Poster Lab
shortDescription: Dynamic foreshortening and energy FX. Poster task: burst background + logo lockup.
fullDescription: Dynamic foreshortening and energy FX. Poster task: burst background + logo lockup.

title: Myth Hero — Nezha (哪吒)
seriesTitle: Fan Art Arcade: Draw & Poster Lab
shortDescription: Ribbons and flame motifs with calligraphic line. Poster task: front + ¾ head studies + emblem badge.
fullDescription: Ribbons and flame motifs with calligraphic line. Poster task: front + ¾ head studies + emblem badge.

title: Anime City Scene — Street Corner
seriesTitle: Fan Art Arcade: Draw & Poster Lab
shortDescription: Simple perspective block-in with signage. Poster task: character inset + street corner background (day/night keys).
fullDescription: Simple perspective block-in with signage. Poster task: character inset + street corner background (day/night keys).

title: Gaming Hero Spotlight — Choose Your Game
seriesTitle: Fan Art Arcade: Draw & Poster Lab
shortDescription: Decode materials (metal/leather/fabric) and UI icon language. Poster task: hero + loot/skill icon strip.
fullDescription: Decode materials (metal/leather/fabric) and UI icon language. Poster task: hero + loot/skill icon strip.

title: Chibi Remix & Expressions
seriesTitle: Fan Art Arcade: Draw & Poster Lab
shortDescription: Shift proportions for cute appeal; build a 6-face expression grid; ink with thick–thin lines. Poster task: chibi group mini-poster.
fullDescription: Shift proportions for cute appeal; build a 6-face expression grid; ink with thick–thin lines. Poster task: chibi group mini-poster.

title: Poster Design — Layout & Typography
seriesTitle: Fan Art Arcade: Draw & Poster Lab
shortDescription: Poster hierarchy and type zones; unify with gradient map/limited palette; add tasteful VFX (glow, rim light). Deliverable: final one-sheet.
fullDescription: Poster hierarchy and type zones; unify with gradient map/limited palette; add tasteful VFX (glow, rim light). Deliverable: final one-sheet.

title: Crossover Finale — Mash-Up Poster
seriesTitle: Fan Art Arcade: Draw & Poster Lab
shortDescription: Combine 2–4 characters from different sessions. Unify scale, overlap, palette, and cast-shadow logic. Deliverable: crossover poster + 1 process strip.
fullDescription: Combine 2–4 characters from different sessions. Unify scale, overlap, palette, and cast-shadow logic. Deliverable: crossover poster + 1 process strip.

title: Mindset, Gesture, and Silhouette
shortDescription: Clarity over detail: warm-ups, line of action, C/S curves, straights vs curves, silhouette tests. Deliverable: a gesture vocabulary sheet + silhouette row.
fullDescription: Clarity over detail: warm-ups, line of action, C/S curves, straights vs curves, silhouette tests. Deliverable: a gesture vocabulary sheet + silhouette row.

title: Proportion & Perspective for Characters
shortDescription: Construct poseable figures: adult/child proportion, the bean, simple forms, foreshortening. Deliverable: proportion chart + one clean mannequin.
fullDescription: Construct poseable figures: adult/child proportion, the bean, simple forms, foreshortening. Deliverable: proportion chart + one clean mannequin.

title: Stance & Sit: Weight and Balance
shortDescription: Make figures stand and sit believably: COG, contrapposto, contact, and ground shadows. Deliverable: pose sheet (best 4–6) labeled for balance.
fullDescription: Make figures stand and sit believably: COG, contrapposto, contact, and ground shadows. Deliverable: pose sheet (best 4–6) labeled for balance.

refs.work: Contrapposto exemplars (Roman copies of Greek works)

title: Walk Cycle (Keys Only)
shortDescription: Understand locomotion with keys (no in-betweens): contact→down→passing→up; spacing and arcs. Deliverable: clean 8-key walk strip with arrows.
fullDescription: Understand locomotion with keys (no in-betweens): contact→down→passing→up; spacing and arcs. Deliverable: clean 8-key walk strip with arrows.

title: Overlap, Follow-Through, and Drag
shortDescription: Add life to hair/cloth/tails: lead vs follow, delay/drag, settle. Deliverable: a 6–9 mini-key strip (jump-land or turn) with timing notes.
fullDescription: Add life to hair/cloth/tails: lead vs follow, delay/drag, settle. Deliverable: a 6–9 mini-key strip (jump-land or turn) with timing notes.

refs.work: Flowing cloth/ribbon dynamics in warrior prints

title: Pose, Mood, and Facial Expression
shortDescription: Communicate emotion: acting beats, eyes/brows/mouth synergy, eyeline, head tilt. Deliverable: 9-face grid + 3 mood poses (clean line).
fullDescription: Communicate emotion: acting beats, eyes/brows/mouth synergy, eyeline, head tilt. Deliverable: 9-face grid + 3 mood poses (clean line).

title: Staging Two Characters + Simple Layout
shortDescription: Show relationships and read beats at a glance: proximity, eyelines, overlap, clear silhouettes; stage in a simple room/street. Deliverable: refined panel in 1–2 point.
fullDescription: Show relationships and read beats at a glance: proximity, eyelines, overlap, clear silhouettes; stage in a simple room/street. Deliverable: refined panel in 1–2 point.

title: Beats to Board: Storyline & Storyboard
shortDescription: Sequence your drawings: 6–9 beats (setup→complication→change→resolution), WS/MS/Close, and value grouping for readability. Deliverable: final storyboard page.
fullDescription: Sequence your drawings: 6–9 beats (setup→complication→change→resolution), WS/MS/Close, and value grouping for readability. Deliverable: final storyboard page.

title: (Optional) Dramatic Layout — 3-Point Perspective
shortDescription: Push camera drama with 3-point perspective and depth via value bands. Deliverable: one dramatic angle layout with your character.
fullDescription: Push camera drama with 3-point perspective and depth via value bands. Deliverable: one dramatic angle layout with your character.

title: (Optional) Cleanup & Mini-Animatic
shortDescription: Professional finish and timing test: clean line hierarchy, light value pass, assemble panels into a short animatic (10–20s).
fullDescription: Professional finish and timing test: clean line hierarchy, light value pass, assemble panels into a short animatic (10–20s).

title: Marker Anime Girl — ¾ Bust with Prop
seriesTitle: Marker Anime Characters on Paper — 3-Class Sprint
shortDescription: Clean ink → flats → cel-shadows from one light arrow, then hair flick/feather, small gradients, and gel-pen highlights. Deliverable: finished A4 bust + 1" swatch strip (skin/hair/clothes).
fullDescription: Clean ink → flats → cel-shadows from one light arrow, then hair flick/feather, small gradients, and gel-pen highlights. Deliverable: finished A4 bust + 1" swatch strip (skin/hair/clothes).

refs.work: Poster works (flat color & contour; hair flow)

title: Chinese Character Boy — Wuxia/Modern Fusion (Half-Body)
seriesTitle: Marker Anime Characters on Paper — 3-Class Sprint
shortDescription: Elegant line + glazing for layered cloth; use warm/cool grays for shadow families and small metal highlights. Deliverable: half-body sheet + 3 fabric tiles (silk/cotton/leather).
fullDescription: Elegant line + glazing for layered cloth; use warm/cool grays for shadow families and small metal highlights. Deliverable: half-body sheet + 3 fabric tiles (silk/cotton/leather).

title: Western/Euro-American Girl — Dynamic Full Figure (Poster Crop)
seriesTitle: Marker Anime Characters on Paper — 3-Class Sprint
shortDescription: Graphic silhouette, bold accent color; flats fast → one strong gray family for cel-shadows → VFX pass (rim light, speed lines). Deliverable: full-figure A4 poster crop + hand-lettered title ribbon.
fullDescription: Graphic silhouette, bold accent color; flats fast → one strong gray family for cel-shadows → VFX pass (rim light, speed lines). Deliverable: full-figure A4 poster crop + hand-lettered title ribbon.

refs.work: Bold icon posters (flat color + punchy silhouette)

title: Procreate: Anime Girl ¾ Bust + Blink/Sparkle Loop
shortDescription: Canvas 2000×2000, 10–12 fps. Layer stack: FX/Highlights/Shadows/Flats/Line/Sketch/BG. Pipeline: thumbnails → clean ink (outer thicker) → flats (3–4 hues) → cel-shadows (one light arrow) → 8–12f blink + sparkle (Animation Assist). Export GIF/MP4 + palette strip.
fullDescription: Canvas 2000×2000, 10–12 fps. Layer stack: FX/Highlights/Shadows/Flats/Line/Sketch/BG. Pipeline: thumbnails → clean ink (outer thicker) → flats (3–4 hues) → cel-shadows (one light arrow) → 8–12f blink + sparkle (Animation Assist). Export GIF/MP4 + palette strip.

title: Procreate: Chinese-Inspired Anime Boy (Half-Body) + Idle Breath/Ribbon Sway
shortDescription: Canvas 2200×1600, 10–12 fps. Gesture/bean → line & flats (indigo/teal + metal accents) → stacked glazes for deep folds. Mini-animation: 6–8f idle breath (subtle torso nudge) + 4–6f ribbon/tassel with delayed overlap; consistent chin/collar shadow. Export loop + callout panel.
fullDescription: Canvas 2200×1600, 10–12 fps. Gesture/bean → line & flats (indigo/teal + metal accents) → stacked glazes for deep folds. Mini-animation: 6–8f idle breath (subtle torso nudge) + 4–6f ribbon/tassel with delayed overlap; consistent chin/collar shadow. Export loop + callout panel.

title: Dresscode: Euro-Anime Girl (Full Figure) + Cape/Camera Move
shortDescription: Pre-prep layered PNGs from Procreate (BG, Cape, HairFront, Head, Torso, ArmL/R, Legs, FX). In Dresscode: 1920×1080, 24 fps, 4–6 s. Keyframes: settle → cape swish (rotate + slight translate, hair tip overlap) → slow push-in/pan → settle; ease in/out, offset secondaries. Export MP4 + rig map.
fullDescription: Pre-prep layered PNGs from Procreate (BG, Cape, HairFront, Head, Torso, ArmL/R, Legs, FX). In Dresscode: 1920×1080, 24 fps, 4–6 s. Keyframes: settle → cape swish (rotate + slight translate, hair tip overlap) → slow push-in/pan → settle; ease in/out, offset secondaries. Export MP4 + rig map.

refs.work: The Adventures of Prince Achmed (layered cut-out staging)
Poster layouts (clear title zones & focal hierarchy)

title: 1-Point Perspective — Interior Depth & Simple Façade
seriesTitle: Architecture Foundation Basic — Perspective → Idea → Paper
shortDescription: Calm depth with a single vanishing point. Draw a hallway/café interior (tables, frames) and a tiny centered-door façade study; add a light marker/pencil value pass.
fullDescription: Calm depth with a single vanishing point. Draw a hallway/café interior (tables, frames) and a tiny centered-door façade study; add a light marker/pencil value pass.

refs.museum: Santa Maria Novella (PD images via museums)

title: 2-Point Perspective — Street Corner & Shopfront
seriesTitle: Architecture Foundation Basic — Perspective → Idea → Paper
shortDescription: Rotate the box for a street corner. Add a shop window, door, canopy, and scale figures; finish with three value bands (sky/building/shadow).
fullDescription: Rotate the box for a street corner. Add a shop window, door, canopy, and scale figures; finish with three value bands (sky/building/shadow).

title: 3-Point Perspective — Tall & Dramatic
seriesTitle: Architecture Foundation Basic — Perspective → Idea → Paper
shortDescription: Place a third vanishing point for an up-shot tower. Establish window-grid rhythm and a bold cast shadow; include a tiny diagram showing your VP placement.
fullDescription: Place a third vanishing point for an up-shot tower. Establish window-grid rhythm and a bold cast shadow; include a tiny diagram showing your VP placement.

title: Reading Classics — From Eye-Candy to Ideas
seriesTitle: Architecture Foundation Basic — Perspective → Idea → Paper
shortDescription: Not a timeline—design takeaways. Make two annotated diagrams per building: 1 massing sketch and 1 rhythm overlay (what you’d ‘steal’ for your own corner design).
fullDescription: Not a timeline—design takeaways. Make two annotated diagrams per building: 1 massing sketch and 1 rhythm overlay (what you’d ‘steal’ for your own corner design).

title: AI Assist — From Pencil to Concept Image (Optional)
seriesTitle: Architecture Foundation Basic — Perspective → Idea → Paper
shortDescription: Photograph your line drawing and test materials/light with a cautious AI pass (mood board, not truth). Present side-by-side pencil vs AI and reflect: keep / change / surprise.
fullDescription: Photograph your line drawing and test materials/light with a cautious AI pass (mood board, not truth). Present side-by-side pencil vs AI and reflect: keep / change / surprise.

title: Paper Sculpture Capstone — From Drawing to Model
seriesTitle: Architecture Foundation Basic — Perspective → Idea → Paper
shortDescription: Translate your design into a cardstock massing model. Draft nets with tabs, assemble safely, add façade rhythm with marker lines or cut-outs, mount on a base card with a drawn pavement and a paper tree.
fullDescription: Translate your design into a cardstock massing model. Draft nets with tabs, assemble safely, add façade rhythm with marker lines or cut-outs, mount on a base card with a drawn pavement and a paper tree.

title: Plein Air Starter: Scout → Pencil Sketch → Watercolor Touch
shortDescription: Learn to scout views, simplify big shapes, and mark light. Thumbnails + one main pencil sketch; optional watercolor washes for sky/ground.
fullDescription: Learn to scout views, simplify big shapes, and mark light. Thumbnails + one main pencil sketch; optional watercolor washes for sky/ground.

refs.museum: Public Domain via Art Institute of Chicago / Met

title: Studio from Photo: Value & Color Map
shortDescription: Use your own photo to build a 3-tone value study and a limited-palette color block. Focus on big shapes and clarity over details.
fullDescription: Use your own photo to build a 3-tone value study and a limited-palette color block. Focus on big shapes and clarity over details.

refs.museum: Public Domain via The Met / Musée d’Orsay

title: Collage Landscape: Shape, Rhythm, and Texture
shortDescription: Design with cut shapes first. Glue bands of sky/ground/mountain/tree, then add selective lines/textures. Finish with a collage landscape plus composition notes.
fullDescription: Design with cut shapes first. Glue bands of sky/ground/mountain/tree, then add selective lines/textures. Finish with a collage landscape plus composition notes.

title: Mixed Media Landscape: Watercolor × Oil Pastel
shortDescription: Express atmosphere with contrast. Watercolor base for sky/ground; oil pastel for resist textures and highlights; markers for focal edges.
fullDescription: Express atmosphere with contrast. Watercolor base for sky/ground; oil pastel for resist textures and highlights; markers for focal edges.

title: Photo Landscape: Shoot → Edit → Print & Pair
shortDescription: Treat photography as landscape art. Shoot 12 frames, edit for clarity, then pair your best photo with a simplified drawn echo for a diptych.
fullDescription: Treat photography as landscape art. Shoot 12 frames, edit for clarity, then pair your best photo with a simplified drawn echo for a diptych.

title: Skull First: Building Strong Portraits
shortDescription: Three-view skull quick sketch (front/side/3/4) using cranial sphere + jaw wedge construction, basic thirds/fifths head divisions, and key bony landmarks (zygoma, brow ridge, nasal aperture, TMJ). Then overlay an “X-ray” skull on an AI-generated three-quarter male portrait to connect structure to likeness and observational portrait sketching, with references to historical skull/head studies by Leonardo da Vinci and Albrecht Dürer.
fullDescription: Three-view skull quick sketch (front/side/3/4) using cranial sphere + jaw wedge construction, basic thirds/fifths head divisions, and key bony landmarks (zygoma, brow ridge, nasal aperture, TMJ). Then overlay an “X-ray” skull on an AI-generated three-quarter male portrait to connect structure to likeness and observational portrait sketching, with references to historical skull/head studies by Leonardo da Vinci and Albrecht Dürer.

refs.work: Studies of the human skull (multiple views/sections)
Vier Bücher von menschlicher Proportion (head proportion plates)
Cours de dessin (skull and cast plates)

title: Muscles & Planes: How Light Reads on the Face
shortDescription: Map major muscle masses and simplify the head into planar blocks; render a 5-step value study from ¾ without heavy blending; design hard/soft/lost edges.
fullDescription: Map major muscle masses and simplify the head into planar blocks; render a 5-step value study from ¾ without heavy blending; design hard/soft/lost edges.

title: Portrait Study — Facial Features (Nose, Lips, Ears & Eyes)
shortDescription: Intro to the five main features as 3D forms: nose tip as sphere with clear front/side planes and cheek connection; lips built from layered in–out curves with upper lip in shadow; ears simplified with straight-line block-in and three value groups; eyes as spheres with lid volume and cast shadow. Applied on front and ¾ head views in Adobe Fresco.
fullDescription: Intro to the five main features as 3D forms: nose tip as sphere with clear front/side planes and cheek connection; lips built from layered in–out curves with upper lip in shadow; ears simplified with straight-line block-in and three value groups; eyes as spheres with lid volume and cast shadow. Applied on front and ¾ head views in Adobe Fresco.

title: Male Portrait Study — 3/4 View Planes & Shading
shortDescription: Block in a male head in slight 3/4 view; separate front and side planes; mass in hair, brows, features, and shoulder as unified darks, then build clean skin values with a sharp HB. Think of the head as a sphere with carved planes, using erased highlights and soft blending to turn the form and keep the portrait three-dimensional.
fullDescription: Block in a male head in slight 3/4 view; separate front and side planes; mass in hair, brows, features, and shoulder as unified darks, then build clean skin values with a sharp HB. Think of the head as a sphere with carved planes, using erased highlights and soft blending to turn the form and keep the portrait three-dimensional.

title: Female Portrait (Graphite) — Edges & Transitions
shortDescription: Prioritize edge control and subtle transitions in a female self-portrait. Use graphite-style brushes to keep skin values light while turning the form; refine the eyelid crease and lip softness; design hair as flowing sheen bands. Front or gentle ¾ view brought to an 80–90% finished state. Demo drawn in Adobe Fresco.
fullDescription: Prioritize edge control and subtle transitions in a female self-portrait. Use graphite-style brushes to keep skin values light while turning the form; refine the eyelid crease and lip softness; design hair as flowing sheen bands. Front or gentle ¾ view brought to an 80–90% finished state. Demo drawn in Adobe Fresco.

title: Head Perspectives & Likeness Strategy
shortDescription: Turn heads convincingly with ball/plane logic; plan likeness via envelope, plumb lines, and angle checks. 9 thumbnail tilts → select one for a 3-value study.
fullDescription: Turn heads convincingly with ball/plane logic; plan likeness via envelope, plumb lines, and angle checks. 9 thumbnail tilts → select one for a 3-value study.

title: Senior Portrait (Graphite) — Planes & Structure
shortDescription: Strong planar read and measured block-in; beard as value mass; hair grouped by value with a few directional accents. ¾ head to 80–90% finish.
fullDescription: Strong planar read and measured block-in; beard as value mass; hair grouped by value with a few directional accents. ¾ head to 80–90% finish.

title: Senior Portrait (Female) — Edges & Transitions
shortDescription: Prioritize edge control and subtle transitions; control the eyelid crease and lip softness; hair as sheen bands. Front or gentle ¾ to 80–90% finish.
fullDescription: Prioritize edge control and subtle transitions; control the eyelid crease and lip softness; hair as sheen bands. Front or gentle ¾ to 80–90% finish.

title: Child & Elder Portrait Cues (Graphite)
shortDescription: Child: larger cranium, lower facial third, big-eye/short-nose, low contrast. Elder: bony landmarks + designed wrinkle rhythms; compress value range thoughtfully. Two half-length studies to 60–70%.
fullDescription: Child: larger cranium, lower facial third, big-eye/short-nose, low contrast. Elder: bony landmarks + designed wrinkle rhythms; compress value range thoughtfully. Two half-length studies to 60–70%.

title: Charcoal Capstone — From Block-In to Finish
shortDescription: Vine lay-in → mass shadow family → wipe to halftone → restate darks → set background value → push hard/soft/lost edges; final accents in compressed charcoal; lift with kneaded/chamois. One finished portfolio portrait + process strip.
fullDescription: Vine lay-in → mass shadow family → wipe to halftone → restate darks → set background value → push hard/soft/lost edges; final accents in compressed charcoal; lift with kneaded/chamois. One finished portfolio portrait + process strip.

title: Rules of Play + Collage Passport
shortDescription: Kick-off: composition with cut paper; constraint play ('7 pieces only'). Make a story collage + thumbnails. Stamp your 'maker passport'.
fullDescription: Kick-off: composition with cut paper; constraint play ('7 pieces only'). Make a story collage + thumbnails. Stamp your 'maker passport'.

title: Printmaking I — Foam Relief & Stamps
shortDescription: Experiment with soft-foam block prints; learn positive vs negative space; build a 4-tile pattern.
fullDescription: Experiment with soft-foam block prints; learn positive vs negative space; build a 4-tile pattern.

title: Paper Cutting & Shadow Box
shortDescription: Create a 3–5 layer paper diorama inside a window frame; explore silhouette and depth.
fullDescription: Create a 3–5 layer paper diorama inside a window frame; explore silhouette and depth.

title: Clay Minis — 'Character Ramen'
shortDescription: Additive modeling with air-dry clay: make a ramen bowl with a character topping (eyes, expressions).
fullDescription: Additive modeling with air-dry clay: make a ramen bowl with a character topping (eyes, expressions).

title: Cardboard Engineering I — Tubes, Tabs & Joinery
shortDescription: Engineer with recycled cardboard: join tubes, tabs, slots into a standing form or lampshade (no electricity).
fullDescription: Engineer with recycled cardboard: join tubes, tabs, slots into a standing form or lampshade (no electricity).

title: Wearable Tissue/Fabric Fashion
shortDescription: Experiment with tissue/cellophane drape and fastenings. Build a collar/cape/headpiece and photo-runway it.
fullDescription: Experiment with tissue/cellophane drape and fastenings. Build a collar/cape/headpiece and photo-runway it.

title: Printmaking II — Monoprint / Gel Plate
shortDescription: Pull textures and ghost prints using monoprint/gel plate (or marker transfers). Build a series of 3 prints.
fullDescription: Pull textures and ghost prints using monoprint/gel plate (or marker transfers). Build a series of 3 prints.

title: Mixed-Media Collagraph (Sustainable Plate)
shortDescription: Build cardboard collagraph plates with string/leaf/fabric; print textures and patterns.
fullDescription: Build cardboard collagraph plates with string/leaf/fabric; print textures and patterns.

title: 3D Basics — Design for 3D Print
shortDescription: Think in volume: design a charm/toy in Tinkercad, export STL, make a proxy paper net. Optional: print, sand, and paint.
fullDescription: Think in volume: design a charm/toy in Tinkercad, export STL, make a proxy paper net. Optional: print, sand, and paint.

title: Diorama Worlds (Collage × Sculpture)
shortDescription: Build a shoe-box diorama: scene + character + title plaque. Focus on foreground/mid/background layers.
fullDescription: Build a shoe-box diorama: scene + character + title plaque. Focus on foreground/mid/background layers.

title: Kinetic Mobile (Calder Spirit)
shortDescription: Make a wire/card mobile balancing recycled shapes; explore rhythm and counterweight.
fullDescription: Make a wire/card mobile balancing recycled shapes; explore rhythm and counterweight.

title: Show Build — Mount, Label, Photograph
shortDescription: Mount and label works, write wall tags, document process photos. Stage a mini-exhibition.
fullDescription: Mount and label works, write wall tags, document process photos. Stage a mini-exhibition.

title: 3D Print II — Finish & Paint
shortDescription: Sand, prime, and paint a printed 3D charm; mount on a display card.
fullDescription: Sand, prime, and paint a printed 3D charm; mount on a display card.

title: Stop-Motion Short (Clay/Collage)
shortDescription: Make a 10–20s stop-motion short using clay/collage and a phone app; add simple lighting and credits.
fullDescription: Make a 10–20s stop-motion short using clay/collage and a phone app; add simple lighting and credits.

title: Recycled Runway or Sound Object
shortDescription: Choose: A) Full wearable look from cardboard/tissue (runway) or B) Percussive sound sculpture (shakers, strings).
fullDescription: Choose: A) Full wearable look from cardboard/tissue (runway) or B) Percussive sound sculpture (shakers, strings).

title: First Marks — Prehistory & Early Ritual Images
shortDescription: Why humans make images: ritual, myth, and naturalism. Lascaux/Chauvet, Tassili, Göbekli Tepe. Mini: charcoal ‘negative hand’ and timeline start.
fullDescription: Why humans make images: ritual, myth, and naturalism. Lascaux/Chauvet, Tassili, Göbekli Tepe. Mini: charcoal ‘negative hand’ and timeline start.

title: Civilizations & Symbols — Egypt · Mesopotamia · Indus · Early China · Olmec
shortDescription: Canon and meaning systems: Narmer Palette, Standard of Ur, Giza triads, Shang bronzes, Olmec heads. Mini: 3-register story using fixed profile canon.
fullDescription: Canon and meaning systems: Narmer Palette, Standard of Ur, Giza triads, Shang bronzes, Olmec heads. Mini: 3-register story using fixed profile canon.

refs.museum: The Egyptian Museum / PD via major museums

title: Classical Balance — Greece & Rome (with Qin/Han Echo)
shortDescription: Ideal body and civic space: Polykleitos, Parthenon frieze, Augustus of Prima Porta, Pantheon; echo: Terracotta Army. Mini: 2-minute contrapposto stick→mannequin.
fullDescription: Ideal body and civic space: Polykleitos, Parthenon frieze, Augustus of Prima Porta, Pantheon; echo: Terracotta Army. Mini: 2-minute contrapposto stick→mannequin.

title: Light of Faith — Byzantine · Early Islamic · Medieval Europe
shortDescription: Icons and mosaics; aniconism and pattern logic. Hagia Sophia, Dome of the Rock, Qur’anic pages, Insular manuscripts. Mini: radial/tessellation tile.
fullDescription: Icons and mosaics; aniconism and pattern logic. Hagia Sophia, Dome of the Rock, Qur’anic pages, Insular manuscripts. Mini: radial/tessellation tile.

title: Vertical Dreams & Misty Mountains — Gothic Europe × Song China
shortDescription: Light/height vs. void/atmosphere: Chartres, flying buttress; Fan Kuan/Ma Yuan. Mini: rose-window radial + misty value-layer thumbnail.
fullDescription: Light/height vs. void/atmosphere: Chartres, flying buttress; Fan Kuan/Ma Yuan. Mini: rose-window radial + misty value-layer thumbnail.

title: Renaissance Revolutions — Perspective · Humanism · Anatomy
shortDescription: 1-point space and human measure: Masaccio, Botticelli, Leonardo, Michelangelo, Raphael. Mini: 1-point room with figure; label horizon/VP.
fullDescription: 1-point space and human measure: Masaccio, Botticelli, Leonardo, Michelangelo, Raphael. Mini: 1-point room with figure; label horizon/VP.

title: After the Balance — Mannerism & Global Exchanges
shortDescription: Elegance and distortion; trade/mission exchanges. Parmigianino, El Greco, Momoyama screens, Mughal miniatures. Mini: exaggerate a Renaissance pose.
fullDescription: Elegance and distortion; trade/mission exchanges. Parmigianino, El Greco, Momoyama screens, Mughal miniatures. Mini: exaggerate a Renaissance pose.

title: Drama & Delight — Baroque and Rococo
shortDescription: Theater vs. playfulness: Caravaggio, Bernini, Velázquez, Rembrandt, Fragonard; Andean/Mexican Baroque note. Mini: 3-tone chiaroscuro notan.
fullDescription: Theater vs. playfulness: Caravaggio, Bernini, Velázquez, Rembrandt, Fragonard; Andean/Mexican Baroque note. Mini: 3-tone chiaroscuro notan.

title: Reason & Storm — Neoclassicism → Romanticism
shortDescription: Revolutionary reason vs. sublime emotion: David/Ingres; Delacroix/Turner/Goya; East Asia echo: Hokusai/Hiroshige. Mini: ‘calm vs. storm’ diptych.
fullDescription: Revolutionary reason vs. sublime emotion: David/Ingres; Delacroix/Turner/Goya; East Asia echo: Hokusai/Hiroshige. Mini: ‘calm vs. storm’ diptych.

title: The Modern Eye I — Realism → Impressionism → Post-Impressionism
shortDescription: Contemporary life to optical color and subjective structure: Courbet, Manet, Monet, Degas, Van Gogh, Gauguin, Cézanne. Mini: warm/cool color study.
fullDescription: Contemporary life to optical color and subjective structure: Courbet, Manet, Monet, Degas, Van Gogh, Gauguin, Cézanne. Mini: warm/cool color study.

title: The Modern Eye II — Early 20th-Century Abstraction & Design
shortDescription: Break the picture: Fauves, Expressionism, Cubist logic (PD-safe: Juan Gris), Futurist motion, De Stijl, Bauhaus. Mini: cubist collage with plane shifts.
fullDescription: Break the picture: Fauves, Expressionism, Cubist logic (PD-safe: Juan Gris), Futurist motion, De Stijl, Bauhaus. Mini: cubist collage with plane shifts.

title: Ideas as Art — Dada → Surrealism → AbEx → Pop · Minimal · Conceptual (to c.1970s)
shortDescription: From anti-art and dream logic to idea-first art. Duchamp; Magritte/Dalí; Rothko/Pollock; Warhol/Lichtenstein; Judd; Kosuth; Smithson. Mini: surreal juxtaposition sketch (consistent shadow) or minimal composition + 2-sentence ‘artist statement’.
fullDescription: From anti-art and dream logic to idea-first art. Duchamp; Magritte/Dalí; Rothko/Pollock; Warhol/Lichtenstein; Judd; Kosuth; Smithson. Mini: surreal juxtaposition sketch (consistent shadow) or minimal composition + 2-sentence ‘artist statement’.

refs.museum: Philadelphia Museum of Art / MoMA (link-out only)

title: Global Modernisms (1900–1970)
shortDescription: Modernity beyond Europe: Mexican Muralism, Harlem Renaissance, Gutai, Indian Progressives. Link-out only (copyrighted images); focus on context and formal analysis.
fullDescription: Modernity beyond Europe: Mexican Muralism, Harlem Renaissance, Gutai, Indian Progressives. Link-out only (copyrighted images); focus on context and formal analysis.

title: Photography & Film Foundations (to c.1940s)
shortDescription: From Niépce and Daguerre to Muybridge and montage. How lens technology reshapes seeing in art and design.
fullDescription: From Niépce and Daguerre to Muybridge and montage. How lens technology reshapes seeing in art and design.

refs.work: View from the Window at Le Gras

title: Design & Architecture Movements (pre-Contemporary)
shortDescription: From Arts & Crafts and Art Nouveau to Bauhaus and early Modernism. Focus on PD-friendly figures (Morris, Horta, Mackintosh, Behrens).
fullDescription: From Arts & Crafts and Art Nouveau to Bauhaus and early Modernism. Focus on PD-friendly figures (Morris, Horta, Mackintosh, Behrens).

title: Sketch with confidence: Line Control Warm-up
shortDescription: Train confident, economical lines: (1) pressure ladder + ghost-then-commit straight/arc lines; (2) one-line contour of a simple object (2 tries); (3) add weight only to load-bearing edges (overlaps, shadow side); (4) annotate a 3-step process strip.
fullDescription: Train confident, economical lines: (1) pressure ladder + ghost-then-commit straight/arc lines; (2) one-line contour of a simple object (2 tries); (3) add weight only to load-bearing edges (overlaps, shadow side); (4) annotate a 3-step process strip.

refs.work: Cottage near the Entrance to a Wood

title: Unlocking Video Art: Read the Deep Structure
shortDescription: Discover the origins of video art, explore its deep structure, and learn how artists use time as a medium.
fullDescription: Discover the origins of video art, explore its deep structure, and learn how artists use time as a medium.
legacyMissionTitle: Unlocking Video Art: Read the Deep Structure

title: Foundation of Sketch Academic
shortDescription: Turn basic shapes into convincing materials with values, edges, and observational control.
fullDescription: Turn basic shapes into convincing materials with values, edges, and observational control.

title: Early Art Exploration (4-7)
shortDescription: A warm and creative online class for young learners to explore art, materials, and imagination.
fullDescription: A warm and creative online class for young learners to explore art, materials, and imagination.

title: Leathercraft Mastery
shortDescription: Step-by-step leather ornaments — shape, carve, and dye. Ideal for focused learners.
fullDescription: Step-by-step leather ornaments — shape, carve, and dye. Ideal for focused learners.

title: Jewelry Design Studio
shortDescription: Design and sketch original jewelry pieces — rings, pendants, and wearable art.
fullDescription: Design and sketch original jewelry pieces — rings, pendants, and wearable art.

title: Custom Design: Costume & Fashion
shortDescription: Learn to design fashion using proportion, perspective, and color.
fullDescription: Learn to design fashion using proportion, perspective, and color.

title: Visual Storytelling: Narrative
seriesTitle: Illustration Lab — From Story to Finished Page
shortDescription: Learn to tell stories through images, panel composition, and interaction.
fullDescription: Learn to tell stories through images, panel composition, and interaction.

title: AI Short-Video Workshop
shortDescription: Learn how to craft AI prompts, remix footage, and design audio for short-form videos.
fullDescription: Learn how to craft AI prompts, remix footage, and design audio for short-form videos.

title: Contemporary Art Aesthetics
shortDescription: Learn what contemporary art is, why it matters, and how to appreciate it.
fullDescription: Learn what contemporary art is, why it matters, and how to appreciate it.
